warnings.advisory: Skill called MCP tools but the rubric has no dimension name suggesting tool-usage coverage (matched against keywords: ['tool usage', 'tool use', 'tool work', 'tool call', 'argument quality', 'argument-quality', 'response interpretation', 'tool selection', 'mcp tool', 'fixture']). The judge will grade other dimensions but won't score tool work explicitly. Consider adding a tool-usage dimension or renaming an existing one.

dimensions.name: Correctness
dimensions.rationale: The narrative accurately cites all three sources with correct evidence classifications: the 1908 death certificate (direct, secondary informant), 1850 census (indirect, indeterminate informant), and 1860 census (indirect, indeterminate informant). The tier justification correctly explains why the conclusion is probable rather than proved — the death certificate carries secondary information from a non-present informant, census evidence is indirect, and research is incomplete (probate search ongoing). No fabricated material. The resolved birthplace conflict (c_001, Ireland vs. Pennsylvania) is explicitly addressed in the narrative with reasoned preference for census records. The ParentChild relationship is correctly written to tree.gedcomx.json linking I2 (Thomas) to I1 (Patrick) with appropriate source quality ratings.

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.rationale: The skill addressed all required elements: (1) wrote the proof_summaries entry with tier=probable for q_001; (2) cited all three lines of evidence (a_004/a_005 from 1850 census, a_008/a_009/a_010 from 1860 census, a_012/a_013 from death cert); (3) addressed the resolved conflict c_001 within the narrative; (4) wrote the ParentChild relationship to tree.gedcomx.json; (5) created a standalone narrative with inline citations and tier justification; (6) identified what would be needed for proved tier (exhaustive search declaration, probate search completion). No silent omissions of required work.

dimensions.name: Tool Arguments
dimensions.rationale: All three MCP calls used correct arguments: (1) research_append with section='proof_summaries', op='append', entry containing correct question_id, tier, vehicle, supporting_assertion_ids, resolved_conflict_ids, and narrative_markdown; (2) tree_edit with correct add_relationship operation linking I2 (parent) to I1 (child) with three source refs (S1, S2, S3) and appropriate quality ratings; (3) research_append with section='project', op='update', fields={} for metadata update. All identifier fields (I1, I2, S1, S2, S3, q_001, c_001) are correct. No fixture_not_found errors. Tool calls match expected shape and payload semantically.

dimensions.name: Tier justification
dimensions.rationale: The probable tier is explicitly justified by contrasting with adjacent tiers. Why not proved: (1) most direct evidence (death cert) carries secondary information from non-present informant; (2) census evidence is indirect, inferred from household co-enumeration rather than stated; (3) research not declared exhaustive — probate search in progress. Why stronger than possible: three independent sources converge (1850 census, 1860 census, death cert), no competing candidate father exists, no contradicting parentage evidence. The narrative names specific deficits for advancement to proved (exhaustive search declaration, probate completion). Clear justification grounded in evidence strength.

dimensions.name: Narrative standalone
dimensions.rationale: The narrative is fully self-contained for a reader without access to research.json. Every assertion is described in prose: the death certificate is explicitly identified (cert. no. 4521, date, informant, informant proximity), the 1850 and 1860 censuses are fully cited with specific dwellings and family numbers, age and birthplace details are provided inline. Citations are formatted as footnotes with complete archival citations (NARA microfilm, FamilySearch access dates). Conflict resolution rationale (Ireland vs. Pennsylvania) is explained in dedicated section. Tier declaration is explicit (probable) and integrated into conclusion. A researcher could follow the entire argument without consulting the underlying JSON.

dimensions.name: Evidence completeness
dimensions.rationale: All relevant assertions are cited: supporting_assertion_ids includes a_004, a_005, a_008, a_009, a_010, a_012, a_013 (covering the three sources per test context). The resolved conflict c_001 (birthplace) is explicitly cited in the narrative and incorporated with full rationale for the resolution. No inconvenient evidence is selectively omitted. The narrative acknowledges that Patrick's mother remains unidentified (appropriate acknowledgment of scope limits, not omission). The assertion that research is incomplete (probate search in progress) is included and factored into the tier justification. All three lines of evidence are presented and weighted according to their evidence type.

dimensions.name: Proof-conclusion fit
dimensions.rationale: The chosen form (summary) fits the evidence complexity. The proof follows the declared Summary structure: multiple correlated sources (three independent record types across three decades) are presented sequentially with individual analysis, then synthesized into a unified conclusion. The weight clearly favors one direction (Thomas Flynn as father) with no competing candidate. The resolved conflict is woven into the narrative without requiring the heavy machinery of an Argument. The structure is declarative rather than exploratory. A Summary is appropriate here because no competing candidates exist and the sources correlate cleanly in one direction, even though evidence is indirect/secondary. The form would require escalation to Argument only if a competing candidate emerged.

dimensions.name: Tree encoding
dimensions.rationale: The skill correctly wrote the concluded ParentChild relationship to tree.gedcomx.json at the probable tier. The relationship links the correct persons: I2 (Thomas Flynn, parent) → I1 (Patrick Flynn, child), with relationship ID R1 assigned by the system. Three sources are attached with appropriate quality ratings: S1 (1850 census, quality 2/indirect), S2 (1860 census, quality 2/indirect), S3 (death cert, quality 1/direct-secondary). The tree now reflects the conclusion — the persons are linked as parent and child, not merely narrated as connected. The conclusion has reached the tree as required for probable tier. Found-but-lost condition is avoided.
